content: I'm really struggling to maintain my routine since my knee injury. I used to do Brazilian jiu-jitsu three times a week, but that's obviously on hold now.
I'm sorry to hear about your injury. That must be frustrating, especially with an activity you enjoy so much. What does your current daily routine look like?
Well, I work from home mostly as a QA lead for a healthcare software company in Austin. My days are pretty sedentary now, which isn't helping my recovery.
Working from home can definitely make it harder to stay active. Have you talked to your physical therapist about exercises you can do at your desk or during breaks?
Yeah, they gave me some PT exercises, but I keep forgetting to do them. I'm usually in back-to-back meetings from 9 to 5. My speedcubing time has also suffered - I used to practice during lunch.
That's a lot of meetings! Maybe you could set calendar reminders for your PT exercises? Even just 5 minutes between meetings could help. And speedcubing is such a fun hobby - I bet you miss it.
I do miss it. I was getting pretty fast too - my personal best is 18 seconds. Now I just listen to podcasts during lunch instead, which isn't quite the same mental break.

18 seconds is impressive! That's serious speedcubing. Podcasts are great, but I can see how they might not give you the same satisfaction. Have you tried any seated exercises that don't strain your knee?
Not really. I've been so focused on just getting through work. My apartment has a small gym, but I haven't been motivated to use it. I used to love the social aspect of BJJ too.

The social connection is such an important part of activities like BJJ. Maybe there are online communities for speedcubing or other hobbies where you could get some of that interaction while you're recovering?
That's a good idea. I've been doing some crossword puzzles in the evenings instead, but it's pretty solitary. My wife keeps encouraging me to join some online gaming groups, but I'm not sure.

Online gaming could be a fun way to connect with people. Or maybe there are virtual speedcubing competitions you could participate in? That way you'd be engaging with your passion while meeting others who share it.

I hadn't thought about virtual competitions. I'll have to look into that. My wife also suggested we start doing aquarium maintenance together - we have a 55-gallon freshwater tank that's been neglected.
That sounds like a perfect project! Aquarium maintenance can be surprisingly therapeutic, and it's something you can do together. Plus, it gets you moving a bit without straining your knee too much.
True. I used to love working on it. Maybe we could make it part of our evening routine - do some maintenance while catching up on our days. It would give me something to look forward to after work.

That's a great plan! Having something enjoyable to transition into after work can really help with the whole day's rhythm. And it's perfect for your recovery since you can sit while working on the tank.
Exactly. I think I'll start tonight. Maybe I'll even challenge myself to a speedcubing session afterward if my knee feels okay. Thanks for helping me think this through!
You're very welcome! It sounds like you've got a solid plan forming. Remember to listen to your body and not push too hard too fast. I'd love to hear how the aquarium project goes!
